 {"id":"phage_explorer-9d7y","title":"Implement WebGL DotPlot","description":"Implement a GPU-accelerated DotPlot for the Web App to handle large genomes instantly.\n\n**Requirements:**\n1. **Shader:** Create a GLSL fragment shader that compares sequence texture positions (x vs y) to output similarity.\n2. **Encoding:** Create a utility to pack DNA sequences into a 1D texture (A=0, C=0.25, etc.).\n3. **Interaction:** Implement pan/zoom uniforms to allow deep exploration of the similarity matrix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-16T19:22:39.498388908-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T21:14:55.839516981-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T21:14:55.839516981-06:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-9d7y","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-9dr4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-16T19:22:39.510275599-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-9dr4","title":"Web App Feature Parity & Ultra Upgrade","description":"## Web App Feature Parity & Ultra Upgrade

This epic tracked the initial Web App parity buildout:
- sql.js-backed SQLite DB load + repository
- Canvas sequence grid (DNA/AA/dual, themes, diff)
- App layout/orchestration + extracted PhageList
- WebGL dot plot + GenomeTrack system
- Worker/WASM fuzzy search
- 3D structure ↔ 2D sequence linking

 {"id":"phage_explorer-9et6","title":"[WEB-P4] Port evolution replay logic","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:02:14.166336-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:42:55.761318-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:42:55.761318-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-9et6","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-6vqr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:13.768837-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"phage_explorer-9fz6","title":"perf(web): NonBDNAOverlay avoid recomputing cruciforms","description":"NonBDNAOverlay currently recomputes heavy cruciform detection whenever the user adjusts G4/Z-DNA thresholds because all analyses are computed in a single useMemo with dependencies [sequence, g4Threshold, zdnaThreshold]. On large genomes this causes obvious UI jank. Fix by splitting memoization: compute cruciforms only when sequence changes; compute G4 list once then filter by threshold; keep zDNA dependent on threshold. Preserve results/behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:15:31.359882556-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T01:19:16.323835061-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T01:19:16.323835061-06:00","close_reason":"Split NonBDNAOverlay analysis memos so cruciform detection only runs on sequence change; fixed overlap filtering to use proper interval intersection."}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-9jc","title":"Advanced: Cocktail Resistance Evolution Simulator","description":"Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using Gillespie/tau-leaping. Params from genome: receptor diversity, Sie genes, CRISPR spacer matches. Live ASCII trajectories, risk meter, side-by-side mono vs cocktail. Turns genomic evidence into dosing/risk guidance. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High (stochastic processes, evolutionary dynamics).","notes":"## COCKTAIL RESISTANCE EVOLUTION SIMULATOR

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using genome-derived parameters (receptor diversity, anti-defense, spacer proximity).

### How to Build

**Stochastic Engine:**
- Gillespie/tau-leaping in Rust+WASM (rand + rayon)
- Events: bacterial growth, phage adsorption, resistance mutation, receptor switching

**Genome-Derived Parameters:**
- Receptor diversity from tail fiber annotation
- Sie genes (superinfection exclusion)
- CRISPR spacer matches (immunity potential)
- Anti-defense gene count

**User Inputs:**
- MOI (multiplicity of infection)
- Dosing cadence (hours between doses)
- Population size (CFU/mL)
- Cocktail composition (1-5 phages)

**Outputs:**
- Probability of resistance over time
- Time-to-resistance distribution
- Compare mono vs cocktail curves
- Optimal cocktail selection

### TUI Features
- Live ASCII trajectories (multiple runs overlaid)
- Risk meter updates in real-time
- Side-by-side mono vs cocktail comparison
- Sliders: MOI, dose interval, population size
- Toggle 'optimize cocktail' to auto-pick best trio
- Resistance probability timeline
- [R]un new simulation, [C]ompare, [O]ptimize

### Why It's Good
- Turns genomic evidence into therapy guidance
- Quantifies resistance risk over time
- Compares mono vs cocktail robustness
- Identifies synergistic combinations

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM for Gillespie simulator (rand, rayon)
- TS/Ink UI for sliders and live trajectories
- SQLite cache for genome-derived parameters
- ASCII braille for trajectory plots

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 9/10","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"OrangeSnow","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:55:45.232996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:12:58.186273297-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:12:58.186273297-06:00","close_reason":"Implemented Gillespie/tau-leaping stochastic simulator for resistance evolution.
